Validation Step: Total row count matches source system record
Notes / Action If Fail: Re-run extraction; verify WHERE clause not accidentally filtering rows

Validation Step: Row count after JOIN does not exceed larger table
SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id;
Notes / Action If Fail: Fan-out from 1-to-many JOIN detected; add DISTINCT or check key cardinality

Validation Step: Row count after LEFT JOIN equals left table exactly (no filter applied)
SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;
Notes / Action If Fail: Unexpected fan-out; audit join key for duplicates on right side

Validation Step: Filtered subset row count is plausible proportion of total
SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'completed';
Notes / Action If Fail: Confirm status values with stakeholder; check for NULL status rows

Validation Step: Aggregated group count matches expected number of categories
Notes / Action If Fail: Extra values may indicate inconsistent formatting; run Category Audit sheet

Validation Step: Date-filtered row count aligns with known reporting period volume
SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN '2023-01-01' AND '2023-12-31';
Notes / Action If Fail: Large deviation warrants date format check and impossible-date audit

Validation Step: Subquery or CTE intermediate result count is reasonable before final SELECT
SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) AS total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) sub;
Notes / Action If Fail: Mismatch means GROUP BY key is not unique identifier; revisit query plan

SQL Template: SELECT SUM(order_amount) FROM orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN '2023-10-01' AND '2023-12-31';
column10: Investigate currency conversion, refunds exclusion, or date boundary logic

Validation Step: Count of active customers in last 90 days
SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM orders WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days';
column10: Check CURRENT_DATE vs. report run date; confirm CRM definition of "active"
Outlier orders skewing AVG; cross-reference with median and run outlier check

SQL Template: SELECT category, SUM(order_amount) FROM orders JOIN products USING(product_id) GROUP BY category ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 1;
column10: Category name mismatch may be formatting inconsistency; run TRIM/UPPER audit

SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN status='refunded' THEN 1 END)1.0/COUNT() FROM orders;
column10: Verify status values include all refund variants (e.g. 'refund_pending')
Products with zero sales are excluded; confirm whether benchmark includes them

SQL Template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers WHERE signup_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-01-31';
column10: Timezone offset on signup_date timestamp may shift boundary rows

Expected Benchmark: Links order to customer; orphan if NULL
Actual Result: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NULL;
column12: Any NULLs here break customer-level aggregation; flag as referential integrity failure

Expected Benchmark: Core revenue metric; NULL silently excluded from SUM
Actual Result: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_amount IS NULL;
column12: SUM ignores NULLs without warning; use COALESCE(order_amount, 0) after investigation

Actual Result: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers WHERE email IS NULL OR TRIM(email) = '';
column12: High NULL rate acceptable if email is optional at signup; document assumption

Actual Result: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_date IS NULL;
column12: NULL dates exclude rows from date-range filters silently; escalate to data engineering

Expected Benchmark: Used in GROUP BY revenue breakdowns
Actual Result: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products WHERE category IS NULL OR TRIM(category) = '';
column12: Uncategorised products create hidden "NULL" group in aggregations; use COALESCE(category, 'Uncategorised')

Expected Benchmark: NULL valid for top-level; unexpected NULLs elsewhere corrupt hierarchy
Actual Result: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE manager_id IS NULL;
column12: Multiple NULLs suggest data load error or org restructure not yet reflected

Expected Benchmark: NULL vs. 0 distinction affects net revenue calc
Actual Result: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_items WHERE discount_amount IS NULL;
column12: Confirm with stakeholder: does NULL mean no discount or data not captured? Document decision

Expected Benchmark: SELECT customer_id, COUNT() FROM customers GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT() > 1;
column9: Use ROW_NUMBER() PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY created_at DESC; keep row 1
